title: Crispy Duck Biryani
description: A flavorful and aromatic Indian-inspired dish featuring crispy-skinned duck in a fragrant rice biryani.
instructions: Pat the duck breast dry with paper towels and season with salt.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and sear the duck breast until browned, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast the duck breast for 12-15 minutes or until cooked through.
In a large saucepan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the ginger paste, garlic paste, cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder, and red chili powder to the saucepan. Cook for 1 minute.
Add 2 cups of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Add the soaked Basmati rice to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan, and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the rice is cooked and fluffy.
Fluff the cooked rice with a fork and stir in some chopped cilantro (optional).
Slice the roasted duck breast and serve with the crispy-skinned duck on top of the flavorful rice biryani.
